| Rank | Domain 1 | Domain 2 | Gap Score | Joint Pubs | Doctrine Alignment |
|------|----------|----------|-----------|------------|-------------------|
| 1 | Beta Cell Regen | Health Equity | 100.0 | 0 | Tier 1 (Epidemiological Data) + Tier 2 |
| 2 | Insulin Resistance | Islet Transplant | 100.0 | 1 | Tier 1 (Clinical Trial Intel) |
| 3 | Islet Transplant | Drug Repurposing | 100.0 | 0 | **Tier 1 × Tier 1** (Drug Repurposing + Clinical Trial Intel) |
| 4 | Islet Transplant | Health Equity | 100.0 | 0 | Tier 1 (Epidemiological Data) |
| 5 | Gene Therapy | LADA | 100.0 | 0 | Tier 2 alignment |

**Key insight:** Gap #3 (Islet Transplant × Drug Repurposing) remains the highest-priority gap for computational screening — it sits at the intersection of two Tier 1 contribution areas. Paper [PMID 41872174] on antigen-specific immunotherapy for islet transplant tolerance may begin to address the adjacent Immunotherapy × Islet Transplant space.

### Additional Gaps Aligned with Tier 1 Areas

## Breaking News

### FDA Approves Awiqli (Insulin Icodec) — March 26, 2026
**This is the biggest development since the last report.** The FDA approved Novo Nordisk's Awiqli (insulin icodec-abae) injection as the **first and only once-weekly basal insulin** for adults with type 2 diabetes. This is a landmark in insulin delivery, reducing injection frequency from daily to weekly. US availability expected in coming months. Directly relevant to our tracked trial NCT07076199 (insulin icodec Phase 3 for T1D) — the T2D approval may accelerate the T1D program.

### Orforglipron FDA Decision Approaching
Eli Lilly's oral GLP-1 candidate orforglipron is on track for a potential US approval decision in early 2026. In trials, orforglipron reduced A1C by 1.3–1.6% and body weight by ~8%. Unlike oral semaglutide, it can be taken with or without food. Our tracked trials NCT06993792 and NCT06972472 are the Phase 3 programs supporting this application.

### CagriSema FDA Decision Expected 2026
Novo Nordisk's CagriSema (cagrilintide + semaglutide) continues toward an FDA decision in 2026 for obesity and type 2 diabetes.

### MUSC Dual-Cell T1D Therapy (Ongoing)
Medical University of South Carolina's two-part therapy combining lab-made insulin-producing cells with engineered immune cells continues development with $1M Breakthrough T1D funding. This approach addresses the immunosuppression problem that limits VX-880. Aligns with our tracked trial NCT04061746 (MUSC MSC therapy, currently recruiting).

### ATTD 2026 Conference (Barcelona, March 11–14)
Conference highlights included advances in closed-loop systems, CGM integration, and cell therapy progress. Breakthrough T1D covered key sessions on T1D treatment transformation.

4. **Review high-priority cross-domain paper [PMID 41872174]** — Antigen-specific immunotherapy for islet transplant tolerance (*Nature Communications*). Directly relevant to Tier 1 Clinical Trial Intelligence and the Islet Transplant × Immunotherapy research gap.

5. **Review Nature Medicine paper [PMID 41872600]** — Diet-microbiome associations in 10,068 individuals. Spans AI/ML + Microbiome domains and may inform Tier 1 Multi-Omics Biomarker Integration work.

6. **Review drug repurposing paper [PMID 41864017]** — Azilsartan repurposing targeting SERPINB9. Evaluate whether this target has diabetes-relevant network connections per Tier 1 Drug Repurposing priorities.

### Upcoming
7. **Monitor orforglipron FDA decision** — Expected imminently. Will significantly impact T2D Novel Therapies category and multiple tracked Phase 3 trials.

8. **Track Awiqli → T1D implications** — With T2D approval in hand, Novo Nordisk's T1D icodec trial (NCT07076199, 877 enrollees) becomes higher priority to monitor for updates.

9. **Consider prioritizing Islet Transplant × Drug Repurposing gap** — Scored 100.0 with 0 joint publications, aligns with two Tier 1 areas. The Nature Communications paper on islet transplant tolerance [PMID 41872174] provides a starting anchor for this work.
